forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KY5QYYC0V75VBPP5T534ZRK0
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8880'
+title: 'Router: session event queue with coalescing, EventEmitting connect, and driver wakeup'
+---
+## What
+
+**Sibling-repo task — work happens in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/FoundationModelsRouter`; build and test there.**
+
+Give routed sessions a queue for tool events, so a long-running command (a test suite, a build) can surface progress and completion to the session without the model polling — and so an *idle* app driver can wake up when background work finishes.
+
+- Add a dependency on `FoundationModelsOperationTool` (lightweight; host → substrate direction — Router knows the `EventEmittingTool`/`OperationEventSink` protocols, tools never know Router).
+- `SessionEventQueue` (actor), owned per `RoutedSession` (`Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Session/RoutedSession.swift`): conforms to `OperationEventSink`. Queue policy: keep every `.completed`; coalesce `.progress` latest-per-`(tool, correlationID)` so a chatty command never stacks stale progress entries. Expose `pendingEvents()` (drain) for the injection task.
+- Connect surface: session-level API to attach `EventEmittingTool`s — `RoutedSession` has no tools parameter today (`makeSession(instructions:workingDirectory:)`), so design where connection happens (e.g. `session.connect(_ tool: any EventEmittingTool)` wiring the tool's sink to this session's queue; research the repo's conventions and note the design in its DESIGN_NOTES equivalent). A fork's queue relationship (shared vs fresh) must be decided and documented — propose fresh-per-session.
+- Driver wakeup: an awaitable surface for the app loop — `nextEvent()` and/or an `AsyncSequence` of events — so a driver whose model turn has ended can start the next turn when an event arrives instead of polling.
+
+Prompt injection (drain into the next `respond` as a text preamble + recorded `PersistableCustomSegment`) is the follow-on task — this task lands the queue, connect, and wakeup.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] A fake `EventEmittingTool` connected to a session delivers events into that session's queue
+- [ ] Coalescing: N `.progress` posts for one correlationID drain as 1 (the latest); interleaved `.completed` events all survive in order
+- [ ] `nextEvent()` suspends while the queue is empty and resumes on the next post; draining is race-free with concurrent posts
+- [ ] Fork behavior decided, documented, and tested
+- [ ] Public API documented to that repo's standards; `swift test` green there
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Unit tests in `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/` — queue policy, connect wiring, `nextEvent` wakeup, concurrent post/drain
+- [ ] `swift test` fully green in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/FoundationModelsRouter`
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -5,31 +5,35 @@
 - 01KY5QYYC0V75VBPP5T534ZRK0
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '8880'
-title: 'Router: session event queue with coalescing, EventEmitting connect, and driver wakeup'
+title: 'Router: session outbox — pending prompt material with ids, coalescing, EventEmitting connect, and driver wakeup'
 ---
 ## What

 **Sibling-repo task — work happens in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/FoundationModelsRouter`; build and test there.**

-Give routed sessions a queue for tool events, so a long-running command (a test suite, a build) can surface progress and completion to the session without the model polling — and so an *idle* app driver can wake up when background work finishes.
+Give routed sessions an **outbox**: a staging area for everything that wants to enter the conversation at a future turn boundary — tool events from long-running work AND queued user prompts — typed in the SDK's own transcript vocabulary. This is deliberately NOT a queue of `Transcript.Entry` (entries are the record of committed turns); it holds *prompt-side material* that only becomes an entry by being sent.

 - Add a dependency on `FoundationModelsOperationTool` (lightweight; host → substrate direction — Router knows the `EventEmittingTool`/`OperationEventSink` protocols, tools never know Router).
-- `SessionEventQueue` (actor), owned per `RoutedSession` (`Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Session/RoutedSession.swift`): conforms to `OperationEventSink`. Queue policy: keep every `.completed`; coalesce `.progress` latest-per-`(tool, correlationID)` so a chatty command never stacks stale progress entries. Expose `pendingEvents()` (drain) for the injection task.
-- Connect surface: session-level API to attach `EventEmittingTool`s — `RoutedSession` has no tools parameter today (`makeSession(instructions:workingDirectory:)`), so design where connection happens (e.g. `session.connect(_ tool: any EventEmittingTool)` wiring the tool's sink to this session's queue; research the repo's conventions and note the design in its DESIGN_NOTES equivalent). A fork's queue relationship (shared vs fresh) must be decided and documented — propose fresh-per-session.
-- Driver wakeup: an awaitable surface for the app loop — `nextEvent()` and/or an `AsyncSequence` of events — so a driver whose model turn has ended can start the next turn when an event arrives instead of polling.
+- `SessionOutbox` (actor), owned per `RoutedSession` (`Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Session/RoutedSession.swift`). Two item kinds, each with a stable id assigned at enqueue:
+  - **Turn-riding segments** — `Transcript.Segment`s that fold into whichever prompt dispatches next. Tool events arrive here: the outbox conforms to `OperationEventSink`; policy: keep every `.completed`, coalesce `.progress` latest-per-`(tool, correlationID)`.
+  - **Turn-starting prompts** — full `Transcript.Prompt`s (queued user messages). Never coalesced; dispatch strictly in enqueue order, one turn each. (Enqueue/edit/cancel/dispatch API is the follow-on task — this task lands the storage, kinds, ids, and drain primitive.)
+- Drain primitive: `drainForDispatch()` returns pending segments (+ optionally the next queued prompt) and marks them committed — called from inside the serial-gated chokepoint by the injection task, so drains never interleave with a concurrent turn. Committed items are gone; `pending()` returns a snapshot of what remains, per kind, with ids.
+- Connect surface: session-level API to attach `EventEmittingTool`s — `RoutedSession` has no tools parameter today (`makeSession(instructions:workingDirectory:)`), so design where connection happens (e.g. `session.connect(_ tool: any EventEmittingTool)` wiring the tool's sink to this session's outbox; follow the repo's conventions and record the design in its design notes). Fork behavior (shared vs fresh outbox) must be decided and documented — propose fresh-per-session.
+- Driver wakeup: an awaitable surface — `nextEvent()` and/or an `AsyncSequence` of outbox activity — so an idle app loop can start a turn when background work completes instead of polling.

-Prompt injection (drain into the next `respond` as a text preamble + recorded `PersistableCustomSegment`) is the follow-on task — this task lands the queue, connect, and wakeup.
+Non-goal (record it): durable on-disk outbox persistence. The payloads are SDK types already round-trippable via `TranscriptEntryMapper`/`CustomSegmentRegistry`, so durability is a natural later extension — note it, don't build it.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A fake `EventEmittingTool` connected to a session delivers events into that session's queue
-- [ ] Coalescing: N `.progress` posts for one correlationID drain as 1 (the latest); interleaved `.completed` events all survive in order
-- [ ] `nextEvent()` suspends while the queue is empty and resumes on the next post; draining is race-free with concurrent posts
+- [ ] A fake `EventEmittingTool` connected to a session delivers events into that session's outbox as turn-riding items
+- [ ] Coalescing: N `.progress` posts for one correlationID pend as 1 (the latest); interleaved `.completed` events all survive in order; queued prompts never coalesce and preserve enqueue order
+- [ ] `pending()` reports items with stable ids and kinds; `drainForDispatch()` commits and empties exactly what it returns, race-free with concurrent posts
+- [ ] `nextEvent()` suspends while the outbox is empty and resumes on the next post
 - [ ] Fork behavior decided, documented, and tested
 - [ ] Public API documented to that repo's standards; `swift test` green there

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Unit tests in `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/` — queue policy, connect wiring, `nextEvent` wakeup, concurrent post/drain
+- [ ] Unit tests in `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/` — kind-specific policies, connect wiring, drain/commit semantics under concurrent post, wakeup, fork behavior
 - [ ] `swift test` fully green in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/FoundationModelsRouter`

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass. #long-running
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

 ---
 ## What

 **Sibling-repo task — work happens in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/FoundationModelsRouter`; build and test there.**

-Give routed sessions an **outbox**: a staging area for everything that wants to enter the conversation at a future turn boundary — tool events from long-running work AND queued user prompts — typed in the SDK's own transcript vocabulary. This is deliberately NOT a queue of `Transcript.Entry` (entries are the record of committed turns); it holds *prompt-side material* that only becomes an entry by being sent.
+Give routed sessions a queue for tool events, so a long-running command (a test suite, a build) can surface progress and completion to the session without the model polling — and so an *idle* app driver can wake up when background work finishes.

 - Add a dependency on `FoundationModelsOperationTool` (lightweight; host → substrate direction — Router knows the `EventEmittingTool`/`OperationEventSink` protocols, tools never know Router).
-- `SessionOutbox` (actor), owned per `RoutedSession` (`Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Session/RoutedSession.swift`). Two item kinds, each with a stable id assigned at enqueue:
-  - **Turn-riding segments** — `Transcript.Segment`s that fold into whichever prompt dispatches next. Tool events arrive here: the outbox conforms to `OperationEventSink`; policy: keep every `.completed`, coalesce `.progress` latest-per-`(tool, correlationID)`.
-  - **Turn-starting prompts** — full `Transcript.Prompt`s (queued user messages). Never coalesced; dispatch strictly in enqueue order, one turn each. (Enqueue/edit/cancel/dispatch API is the follow-on task — this task lands the storage, kinds, ids, and drain primitive.)
-- Drain primitive: `drainForDispatch()` returns pending segments (+ optionally the next queued prompt) and marks them committed — called from inside the serial-gated chokepoint by the injection task, so drains never interleave with a concurrent turn. Committed items are gone; `pending()` returns a snapshot of what remains, per kind, with ids.
-- Connect surface: session-level API to attach `EventEmittingTool`s — `RoutedSession` has no tools parameter today (`makeSession(instructions:workingDirectory:)`), so design where connection happens (e.g. `session.connect(_ tool: any EventEmittingTool)` wiring the tool's sink to this session's outbox; follow the repo's conventions and record the design in its design notes). Fork behavior (shared vs fresh outbox) must be decided and documented — propose fresh-per-session.
-- Driver wakeup: an awaitable surface — `nextEvent()` and/or an `AsyncSequence` of outbox activity — so an idle app loop can start a turn when background work completes instead of polling.
+- `SessionEventQueue` (actor), owned per `RoutedSession` (`Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Session/RoutedSession.swift`): conforms to `OperationEventSink`. Queue policy: keep every `.completed`; coalesce `.progress` latest-per-`(tool, correlationID)` so a chatty command never stacks stale progress entries. Expose `pendingEvents()` (drain) for the injection task.
+- Connect surface: session-level API to attach `EventEmittingTool`s — `RoutedSession` has no tools parameter today (`makeSession(instructions:workingDirectory:)`), so design where connection happens (e.g. `session.connect(_ tool: any EventEmittingTool)` wiring the tool's sink to this session's queue; research the repo's conventions and note the design in its DESIGN_NOTES equivalent). A fork's queue relationship (shared vs fresh) must be decided and documented — propose fresh-per-session.
+- Driver wakeup: an awaitable surface for the app loop — `nextEvent()` and/or an `AsyncSequence` of events — so a driver whose model turn has ended can start the next turn when an event arrives instead of polling.

-Non-goal (record it): durable on-disk outbox persistence. The payloads are SDK types already round-trippable via `TranscriptEntryMapper`/`CustomSegmentRegistry`, so durability is a natural later extension — note it, don't build it.
+Prompt injection (drain into the next `respond` as a text preamble + recorded `PersistableCustomSegment`) is the follow-on task — this task lands the queue, connect, and wakeup.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A fake `EventEmittingTool` connected to a session delivers events into that session's outbox as turn-riding items
-- [ ] Coalescing: N `.progress` posts for one correlationID pend as 1 (the latest); interleaved `.completed` events all survive in order; queued prompts never coalesce and preserve enqueue order
-- [ ] `pending()` reports items with stable ids and kinds; `drainForDispatch()` commits and empties exactly what it returns, race-free with concurrent posts
-- [ ] `nextEvent()` suspends while the outbox is empty and resumes on the next post
+- [ ] A fake `EventEmittingTool` connected to a session delivers events into that session's queue
+- [ ] Coalescing: N `.progress` posts for one correlationID drain as 1 (the latest); interleaved `.completed` events all survive in order
+- [ ] `nextEvent()` suspends while the queue is empty and resumes on the next post; draining is race-free with concurrent posts
 - [ ] Fork behavior decided, documented, and tested
 - [ ] Public API documented to that repo's standards; `swift test` green there

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Unit tests in `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/` — kind-specific policies, connect wiring, drain/commit semantics under concurrent post, wakeup, fork behavior
+- [ ] Unit tests in `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/` — queue policy, connect wiring, `nextEvent` wakeup, concurrent post/drain
 - [ ] `swift test` fully green in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/FoundationModelsRouter`

 ## Workflow

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -11,20 +11,22 @@

 **Sibling-repo task — work happens in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/FoundationModelsRouter`; build and test there.**

-Give routed sessions an **outbox**: a staging area for everything that wants to enter the conversation at a future turn boundary — tool events from long-running work AND queued user prompts — typed in the SDK's own transcript vocabulary. This is deliberately NOT a queue of `Transcript.Entry` (entries are the record of committed turns); it holds *prompt-side material* that only becomes an entry by being sent.
+Give routed sessions an **outbox**: a staging area for everything that wants to enter the conversation at a future turn boundary — tool events from long-running work AND queued user prompts. This is deliberately NOT a queue of `Transcript.Entry` (entries are the record of committed turns); it holds *prompt-side material* that only becomes an entry by being sent.
+
+Type layering (pinned): tools post `OperationEvent`s — plain host-neutral data, no transcript types — through `OperationEventSink`. The outbox stores those events natively (coalescing operates on their typed fields). Transcript vocabulary appears in the outbox only for queued user prompts (`Transcript.Prompt`); events are converted to `Transcript.Segment`s later, at dispatch, by the injection task — events are always passengers in some Prompt, never entries or prompts of their own.

 - Add a dependency on `FoundationModelsOperationTool` (lightweight; host → substrate direction — Router knows the `EventEmittingTool`/`OperationEventSink` protocols, tools never know Router).
 - `SessionOutbox` (actor), owned per `RoutedSession` (`Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Session/RoutedSession.swift`). Two item kinds, each with a stable id assigned at enqueue:
-  - **Turn-riding segments** — `Transcript.Segment`s that fold into whichever prompt dispatches next. Tool events arrive here: the outbox conforms to `OperationEventSink`; policy: keep every `.completed`, coalesce `.progress` latest-per-`(tool, correlationID)`.
-  - **Turn-starting prompts** — full `Transcript.Prompt`s (queued user messages). Never coalesced; dispatch strictly in enqueue order, one turn each. (Enqueue/edit/cancel/dispatch API is the follow-on task — this task lands the storage, kinds, ids, and drain primitive.)
-- Drain primitive: `drainForDispatch()` returns pending segments (+ optionally the next queued prompt) and marks them committed — called from inside the serial-gated chokepoint by the injection task, so drains never interleave with a concurrent turn. Committed items are gone; `pending()` returns a snapshot of what remains, per kind, with ids.
+  - **Turn-riding events** — `OperationEvent`s that will fold into whichever prompt dispatches next. The outbox conforms to `OperationEventSink`; policy: keep every `.completed`, coalesce `.progress` latest-per-`(tool, correlationID)`.
+  - **Turn-starting prompts** — full `Transcript.Prompt`s (queued user messages). Never coalesced; dispatch strictly in enqueue order, one turn each. (Enqueue/edit/cancel/dispatch API is the follow-on prompt-queue task — this task lands the storage, kinds, ids, and drain primitive.)
+- Drain primitive: `drainForDispatch()` returns pending events (+ optionally the next queued prompt) and marks them committed — called from inside the serial-gated chokepoint by the injection task, so drains never interleave with a concurrent turn. Committed items are gone; `pending()` returns a snapshot of what remains, per kind, with ids.
 - Connect surface: session-level API to attach `EventEmittingTool`s — `RoutedSession` has no tools parameter today (`makeSession(instructions:workingDirectory:)`), so design where connection happens (e.g. `session.connect(_ tool: any EventEmittingTool)` wiring the tool's sink to this session's outbox; follow the repo's conventions and record the design in its design notes). Fork behavior (shared vs fresh outbox) must be decided and documented — propose fresh-per-session.
 - Driver wakeup: an awaitable surface — `nextEvent()` and/or an `AsyncSequence` of outbox activity — so an idle app loop can start a turn when background work completes instead of polling.

-Non-goal (record it): durable on-disk outbox persistence. The payloads are SDK types already round-trippable via `TranscriptEntryMapper`/`CustomSegmentRegistry`, so durability is a natural later extension — note it, don't build it.
+Non-goal (record it): durable on-disk outbox persistence. Queued prompts are SDK types and events are Codable — both round-trippable via `TranscriptEntryMapper`/`CustomSegmentRegistry` machinery — so durability is a natural later extension; note it, don't build it.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A fake `EventEmittingTool` connected to a session delivers events into that session's outbox as turn-riding items
+- [ ] A fake `EventEmittingTool` connected to a session delivers `OperationEvent`s into that session's outbox as turn-riding items (stored as events, not transcript types)
 - [ ] Coalescing: N `.progress` posts for one correlationID pend as 1 (the latest); interleaved `.completed` events all survive in order; queued prompts never coalesce and preserve enqueue order
 - [ ] `pending()` reports items with stable ids and kinds; `drainForDispatch()` commits and empties exactly what it returns, race-free with concurrent posts
 - [ ] `nextEvent()` suspends while the outbox is empty and resumes on the next post

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -16,26 +16,28 @@
 Type layering (pinned): tools post `OperationEvent`s — plain host-neutral data, no transcript types — through `OperationEventSink`. The outbox stores those events natively (coalescing operates on their typed fields). Transcript vocabulary appears in the outbox only for queued user prompts (`Transcript.Prompt`); events are converted to `Transcript.Segment`s later, at dispatch, by the injection task — events are always passengers in some Prompt, never entries or prompts of their own.

 - Add a dependency on `FoundationModelsOperationTool` (lightweight; host → substrate direction — Router knows the `EventEmittingTool`/`OperationEventSink` protocols, tools never know Router).
+- **Tools parameter + automatic connection (pinned — no manual connect API):** `makeSession` gains a `tools: [any Tool]` parameter (mirroring Apple's `LanguageModelSession(tools:)`), threaded through `RoutedModel.makeSession` → `LanguageModelSessionBackend` → the underlying `LanguageModelSession`, so the model can call the tools. During session construction, conformance discovery wires event emitters to this session's outbox: `for tool in tools { (tool as? any EventEmittingTool)?.connect(outbox) }`. Nobody remembers to connect — implementing the protocol IS the subscription. A tool instance connects to one sink (tool-per-session; `ShellTool.make()` is per-session-state anyway — note in design docs, don't build sink fan-out speculatively).
 - `SessionOutbox` (actor), owned per `RoutedSession` (`Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Session/RoutedSession.swift`). Two item kinds, each with a stable id assigned at enqueue:
   - **Turn-riding events** — `OperationEvent`s that will fold into whichever prompt dispatches next. The outbox conforms to `OperationEventSink`; policy: keep every `.completed`, coalesce `.progress` latest-per-`(tool, correlationID)`.
   - **Turn-starting prompts** — full `Transcript.Prompt`s (queued user messages). Never coalesced; dispatch strictly in enqueue order, one turn each. (Enqueue/edit/cancel/dispatch API is the follow-on prompt-queue task — this task lands the storage, kinds, ids, and drain primitive.)
 - Drain primitive: `drainForDispatch()` returns pending events (+ optionally the next queued prompt) and marks them committed — called from inside the serial-gated chokepoint by the injection task, so drains never interleave with a concurrent turn. Committed items are gone; `pending()` returns a snapshot of what remains, per kind, with ids.
-- Connect surface: session-level API to attach `EventEmittingTool`s — `RoutedSession` has no tools parameter today (`makeSession(instructions:workingDirectory:)`), so design where connection happens (e.g. `session.connect(_ tool: any EventEmittingTool)` wiring the tool's sink to this session's outbox; follow the repo's conventions and record the design in its design notes). Fork behavior (shared vs fresh outbox) must be decided and documented — propose fresh-per-session.
+- Fork behavior: forks inherit the parent's tool list through construction, and with fresh-per-session outboxes a fork's emitting tools connect to the *fork's* outbox — decide, document, and test (propose fresh-per-session).
 - Driver wakeup: an awaitable surface — `nextEvent()` and/or an `AsyncSequence` of outbox activity — so an idle app loop can start a turn when background work completes instead of polling.

 Non-goal (record it): durable on-disk outbox persistence. Queued prompts are SDK types and events are Codable — both round-trippable via `TranscriptEntryMapper`/`CustomSegmentRegistry` machinery — so durability is a natural later extension; note it, don't build it.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A fake `EventEmittingTool` connected to a session delivers `OperationEvent`s into that session's outbox as turn-riding items (stored as events, not transcript types)
+- [ ] `makeSession(tools:)` threads the tool list into the underlying `LanguageModelSession` (model can call them) AND auto-connects every `EventEmittingTool` to the session's outbox — a fake emitting tool passed in `tools:` delivers events with no explicit connect call anywhere
+- [ ] A tool that does not conform to `EventEmittingTool` passes through untouched (mixed tool lists work)
 - [ ] Coalescing: N `.progress` posts for one correlationID pend as 1 (the latest); interleaved `.completed` events all survive in order; queued prompts never coalesce and preserve enqueue order
 - [ ] `pending()` reports items with stable ids and kinds; `drainForDispatch()` commits and empties exactly what it returns, race-free with concurrent posts
 - [ ] `nextEvent()` suspends while the outbox is empty and resumes on the next post
-- [ ] Fork behavior decided, documented, and tested
+- [ ] Fork behavior: a fork's emitting tools feed the fork's own outbox — decided, documented, tested
 - [ ] Public API documented to that repo's standards; `swift test` green there

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Unit tests in `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/` — kind-specific policies, connect wiring, drain/commit semantics under concurrent post, wakeup, fork behavior
+- [ ] Unit tests in `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/` — auto-connect via `makeSession(tools:)`, mixed tool lists, kind-specific policies, drain/commit semantics under concurrent post, wakeup, fork behavior
 - [ ] `swift test` fully green in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/FoundationModelsRouter`

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass. #long-running
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,42 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on: []
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8880'
+title: 'Router: session outbox — pending prompt material with ids, coalescing, EventEmitting connect, and driver wakeup'
+---
+## What
+
+**Sibling-repo task — work happens in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/FoundationModelsRouter`; build and test there.**
+
+Give routed sessions an **outbox**: a staging area for everything that wants to enter the conversation at a future turn boundary — tool events from long-running work AND queued user prompts. This is deliberately NOT a queue of `Transcript.Entry` (entries are the record of committed turns); it holds *prompt-side material* that only becomes an entry by being sent.
+
+Type layering (pinned): tools post `OperationEvent`s — plain host-neutral data, no transcript types — through `OperationEventSink`. The outbox stores those events natively (coalescing operates on their typed fields). Transcript vocabulary appears in the outbox only for queued user prompts (`Transcript.Prompt`); events are converted to `Transcript.Segment`s later, at dispatch, by the injection task — events are always passengers in some Prompt, never entries or prompts of their own.
+
+- Add a dependency on `FoundationModelsOperationTool` (lightweight; host → substrate direction — Router knows the `EventEmittingTool`/`OperationEventSink` protocols, tools never know Router).
+- **Tools parameter + automatic connection (pinned — no manual connect API):** `makeSession` gains a `tools: [any Tool]` parameter (mirroring Apple's `LanguageModelSession(tools:)`), threaded through `RoutedModel.makeSession` → `LanguageModelSessionBackend` → the underlying `LanguageModelSession`, so the model can call the tools. During session construction, conformance discovery wires event emitters to this session's outbox: `for tool in tools { (tool as? any EventEmittingTool)?.connect(outbox) }`. Nobody remembers to connect — implementing the protocol IS the subscription. A tool instance connects to one sink (tool-per-session; `ShellTool.make()` is per-session-state anyway — note in design docs, don't build sink fan-out speculatively).
+- `SessionOutbox` (actor), owned per `RoutedSession` (`Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Session/RoutedSession.swift`). Two item kinds, each with a stable id assigned at enqueue:
+  - **Turn-riding events** — `OperationEvent`s that will fold into whichever prompt dispatches next. The outbox conforms to `OperationEventSink`; policy: keep every `.completed`, coalesce `.progress` latest-per-`(tool, correlationID)`.
+  - **Turn-starting prompts** — full `Transcript.Prompt`s (queued user messages). Never coalesced; dispatch strictly in enqueue order, one turn each. (Enqueue/edit/cancel/dispatch API is the follow-on prompt-queue task — this task lands the storage, kinds, ids, and drain primitive.)
+- Drain primitive: `drainForDispatch()` returns pending events (+ optionally the next queued prompt) and marks them committed — called from inside the serial-gated chokepoint by the injection task, so drains never interleave with a concurrent turn. Committed items are gone; `pending()` returns a snapshot of what remains, per kind, with ids.
+- Fork behavior: forks inherit the parent's tool list through construction, and with fresh-per-session outboxes a fork's emitting tools connect to the *fork's* outbox — decide, document, and test (propose fresh-per-session).
+- Driver wakeup: an awaitable surface — `nextEvent()` and/or an `AsyncSequence` of outbox activity — so an idle app loop can start a turn when background work completes instead of polling.
+
+Non-goal (record it): durable on-disk outbox persistence. Queued prompts are SDK types and events are Codable — both round-trippable via `TranscriptEntryMapper`/`CustomSegmentRegistry` machinery — so durability is a natural later extension; note it, don't build it.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `makeSession(tools:)` threads the tool list into the underlying `LanguageModelSession` (model can call them) AND auto-connects every `EventEmittingTool` to the session's outbox — a fake emitting tool passed in `tools:` delivers events with no explicit connect call anywhere
+- [ ] A tool that does not conform to `EventEmittingTool` passes through untouched (mixed tool lists work)
+- [ ] Coalescing: N `.progress` posts for one correlationID pend as 1 (the latest); interleaved `.completed` events all survive in order; queued prompts never coalesce and preserve enqueue order
+- [ ] `pending()` reports items with stable ids and kinds; `drainForDispatch()` commits and empties exactly what it returns, race-free with concurrent posts
+- [ ] `nextEvent()` suspends while the outbox is empty and resumes on the next post
+- [ ] Fork behavior: a fork's emitting tools feed the fork's own outbox — decided, documented, tested
+- [ ] Public API documented to that repo's standards; `swift test` green there
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Unit tests in `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/` — auto-connect via `makeSession(tools:)`, mixed tool lists, kind-specific policies, drain/commit semantics under concurrent post, wakeup, fork behavior
+- [ ] `swift test` fully green in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/FoundationModelsRouter`
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file
